author: lionjiadong
text: After creating a new task, all previous tasks are not running

author: lionjiadong
text:
### Summary:

Include a *brief* descrioption of the problem here, and fill out the version info below.

* Celery Version: 5.05
* Celery-Beat Version: 2.2

### Exact steps to reproduce the issue:

1. in django admin add three task  
   [![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48214591/114005265-f69b8800-9891-11eb-9c10-63633d12d662.png)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48214591/114005265-f69b8800-9891-11eb-9c10-63633d12d662.png)  
   interval\_test, interval\_test2, interval\_test3
2. Every time you create one, you will find that the previously created task will no longer run  
   [![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48214591/114005485-29458080-9892-11eb-9819-8111ac139591.png)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48214591/114005485-29458080-9892-11eb-9819-8111ac139591.png)  
   interval测试 : Normal operation after creation,  
   interval测试2 : interval测试 does not run after creation  
   interval测试3 : interval测试 and interval测试2 does not run after creation

author: auvipy
text: I am not sure its a celery issue or not

author: lionjiadong
text:
> I am not sure its a celery issue or not

There will be problems with the database management plan

author: yashikakhurana
text: I faced the same issue when I was trying to inherit the PeriodicTask. Can you provide more insights, may be I could help then.

author: lionjiadong
text:
> 当我尝试继承PeriodicTask时，我遇到了同样的问题。您能否提供更多见解，也许可以帮助您。

I reset the last\_run\_at of all tasks every time I create or modify a task to ensure that each task will be executed as scheduled

author: yashikakhurana
text: have you tried restarting celery whenever you modify or create something?

author: yashikakhurana
text: FYI my issue was resolved using `signals.pre_delete() and signals.pre_save().`
